title: How much emergency fund should I keep?
question_body: Community question about how much emergency fund should i keep?
answer_body: If your job is unstable or your expenses are irregular, choose the larger end of the emergency fund range.

title: How much emergency fund should I keep?
question_body: Community question about how much emergency fund should i keep?
answer_body: A common rule is three to six months of essential expenses. Keep it liquid in a high-yield savings account rather than volatile investments.
combined_text: How much emergency fund should I keep? Community question about how much emergency fund should i keep? A common rule is three to six months of essential expenses. Keep it liquid in a high-yield savings account rather than volatile investments. emergency-fund saving budgeting

title: Should I pay off credit card debt before investing?
question_body: Community question about should i pay off credit card debt before investing?
answer_body: You can contribute enough to get an employer match, but beyond that prioritize the card balance and build a small emergency fund.
combined_text: Should I pay off credit card debt before investing? Community question about should i pay off credit card debt before investing? You can contribute enough to get an employer match, but beyond that prioritize the card balance and build a small emergency fund. credit-card debt investing

title: How can I improve my credit score?
question_body: Community question about how can i improve my credit score?
answer_body: Pay on time, keep utilization low, avoid unnecessary applications, and check reports for errors.
combined_text: How can I improve my credit score? Community question about how can i improve my credit score? Pay on time, keep utilization low, avoid unnecessary applications, and check reports for errors. credit-score credit-card debt

title: How are capital gains taxed?
question_body: Community question about how are capital gains taxed?
answer_body: Capital gains tax depends on holding period, income, jurisdiction and cost basis. Keep records and separate short-term from long-term gains.
combined_text: How are capital gains taxed? Community question about how are capital gains taxed? Capital gains tax depends on holding period, income, jurisdiction and cost basis. Keep records and separate short-term from long-term gains. tax capital-gains investing

title: Should I keep cash or buy bonds for short-term savings?
question_body: Community question about should i keep cash or buy bonds for short-term savings?
answer_body: For short horizons, safety and liquidity matter more than yield. Treasury bills or high-yield savings can fit depending on access needs.

title: Should I refinance my mortgage?
question_body: Community question about should i refinance my mortgage?
answer_body: Refinance when the interest savings exceed closing costs and you expect to keep the loan long enough to break even.
combined_text: Should I refinance my mortgage? Community question about should i refinance my mortgage? Refinance when the interest savings exceed closing costs and you expect to keep the loan long enough to break even. mortgage refinance interest-rate

title: How are capital gains taxed?
question_body: Community question about how are capital gains taxed?
answer_body: Losses may offset gains, but wash sale rules and local tax law can change the result. Consult a tax professional for large transactions.

title: Should I invest in ETFs or individual stocks?
question_body: Community question about should i invest in etfs or individual stocks?
answer_body: Individual stocks can be appropriate if you understand concentration risk, portfolio sizing and tax implications.

title: Should I invest in ETFs or individual stocks?
question_body: Community question about should i invest in etfs or individual stocks?
answer_body: For most investors, diversified low-cost ETFs reduce idiosyncratic risk and require less monitoring than individual stocks.
